name: azure-storage-file-datalake-py
description: A comprehensive skill for working with Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 using the Python SDK. Provides capabilities for file system operations, directory management, file uploads and downloads, access control, and data processing workflows.
capabilities: Create and manage Azure Data Lake Gen2 file systems and directories
Upload, download, and manage files in Azure Data Lake Storage
Configure and manage access control lists (ACLs) and permissions
Work with hierarchical namespace features for HDFS-compatible operations
Execute common data lake workflows including batch operations and data movement
Generate Python code for Azure Data Lake Storage operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The skill provides descriptions and context for Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 SDK usage without executing any operations. Risk score: 0/100.
valueStatement: This skill enables Claude to interact with Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 using the Python SDK, providing file operations, directory management, and access control capabilities for cloud data workflows.
userTitle: Work with Azure Data Lake Storage in Python
limitations: Does not execute actual Azure operations - provides guidance and code generation only
Requires valid Azure credentials and subscription for real operations
Does not handle Azure subscription billing or quota management
No direct support for real-time streaming data ingestion

bestPractices: Always enable hierarchical namespace when creating new Data Lake accounts for HDFS compatibility
Use managed identities or key vault for credential management instead of hardcoding secrets
Implement proper error handling and retry logic for production data operations
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode Azure storage account keys in source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid granting overly broad permissions - follow principle of least privilege for ACLs
Do not upload sensitive data without enabling encryption at rest and using proper access controls

faq.question: What is Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2?
faq.answer: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 is a cloud storage service built on Azure Blob Storage that provides file system semantics, security, and scalability for big data analytics workloads. It combines the capabilities of Azure Blob Storage with Data Lake features including hierarchical namespace and POSIX-compliant access control.

faq.question: How does this skill differ from Azure Blob Storage?
faq.answer: While Blob Storage is general-purpose object storage, Data Lake Gen2 adds hierarchical namespace support for HDFS-compatible directory structures, POSIX-style ACLs, and optimized performance for analytics workloads. Use Data Lake Gen2 for big data analytics; use Blob Storage for general object storage needs.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill generates Python code and provides guidance for Azure Data Lake operations. To execute the code, you need a valid Azure subscription with a Data Lake Storage Gen2 account. The skill itself does not require Azure credentials.

faq.question: Can this skill execute actual data operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates Python code and provides guidance. It does not execute operations against Azure. You will need to run the generated code in your own environment with proper Azure credentials.

faq.question: What Python packages are required?
faq.answer: The primary package is azure-storage-file-datalake. You may also need azure-identity for authentication. Install with: pip install azure-storage-file-datalake azure-identity

faq.question: How do I handle authentication securely?
faq.answer: Use Azure Managed Identities when running in Azure, Azure AD service principals for applications, or SAS tokens for limited access. Never store account keys in code. Use environment variables or Azure Key Vault for credential storage.
